definition: Best observed DES within each (DOI, analyte) group
potential_advantage: Matches study-specific recommendation structure recoverable from literature
main_limitation_in_this_dataset: Conditional on candidate set and reported context

definition: Label top k candidates within each group as positive
potential_advantage: May reduce label sparsity in groups with several similar candidates
main_limitation_in_this_dataset: Many groups have one candidate or one dominant optimum; may create trivial positives in small groups

definition: Label systems within a fixed recovery margin of the best as positive
potential_advantage: Allows near-optimal systems to be treated as acceptable
main_limitation_in_this_dataset: Requires comparable recovery values across standardized conditions; difficult across heterogeneous studies

potential_advantage: Uses more information than binary labels
main_limitation_in_this_dataset: Recovery depends on matrix, pH, water content, extraction time, temperature, and other metadata not consistently available

definition: Define pairwise preferences from recovery differences
main_limitation_in_this_dataset: Requires sufficient within-group comparisons and reliable recovery differences; too few informative groups
